briefdescription.para: Checks whether a block is marked as bad.

detaileddescription.para: As input it takes the address of the block (off_t *). As output it returns flash_block_status (enum flash_block_status *).

briefdescription.para: Marks a block as bad.

detaileddescription.para: As input it takes the address of the block (off_t *). There is no output.

briefdescription.para: Enumeration for extra flash operations.
Block is functional.
Block is marked as bad.
Enumeration for flash block status.

memberdef.definition: typedef bool(* flash_page_cb) (const struct flash_pages_info *info, void *data)
memberdef.name: flash_page_cb

briefdescription.para: Callback type for iterating over flash pages present on a device.

detaileddescription.para: The callback should return true to continue iterating, and false to halt.

simplesect.para: True to continue iteration, false to halt iteration.

briefdescription.para: Parser for flash_parameters for retrieving erase capabilities.

detaileddescription.para: The functions parses flash_parameters type object and returns combination of erase capabilities of 0 if device does not have any. Not that in some cases availability of erase may be dependent on driver options, so even if by hardware design a device provides some erase capabilities, the function may return 0 if these been disabled or not implemented by driver.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, off_t offset, void *data, size_t len)
memberdef.name: flash_read

briefdescription.para: Read data from flash.

detaileddescription.para: All flash drivers support reads without alignment restrictions on the read offset, the read size, or the destination address.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, negative errno code on fail.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, off_t offset, const void *data, size_t len)
memberdef.name: flash_write

briefdescription.para: Write buffer into flash memory.

detaileddescription.para: All flash drivers support a source buffer located either in RAM or SoC flash, without alignment restrictions on the source address. Write size and offset must be multiples of the minimum write block size supported by the driver.
Any necessary write protection management is performed by the driver write implementation itself.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, negative errno code on fail.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, off_t offset, size_t size)
memberdef.name: flash_erase

briefdescription.para: Erase part or all of a flash memory.

detaileddescription.para: Acceptable values of erase size and offset are subject to hardware-specific multiples of page size and offset. Please check the API implemented by the underlying sub driver, for example by using flash_get_page_info_by_offs() if that is supported by your flash driver.
Any necessary erase protection management is performed by the driver erase implementation itself.

The function should be used only for devices that are really explicit erase devices; in case when code relies on erasing device, i.e. setting it to erase-value, prior to some operations, but should work with explicit erase and RAM non-volatile devices, then flash_flatten should rather be used.

parameterdescription.para: : size of area to be erased

simplesect.para: 0 on success, negative errno code on fail.

briefdescription.para: Get device size in bytes.

detaileddescription.para: Returns total logical device size in bytes. Not all devices may support returning size, specifically those with non uniform page layouts or banked, in which case the function will return -ENOTSUP, and user has to rely on Flash page layout functions enabled by CONFIG_FLASH_PAGE_LAYOUT.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, negative errno code on error.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint8_t val, off_t offset, size_t size)
memberdef.name: flash_fill

briefdescription.para: Fill selected range of device with specified value.

detaileddescription.para: Utility function that allows to fill specified range on a device with provided value. The offset and size of range need to be aligned to a write block size of a device.

parameterdescription.para: : value to use for filling the range
: offset of the range to fill

simplesect.para: 0 on success, negative errno code on fail.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, off_t offset, size_t size)
memberdef.name: flash_flatten

briefdescription.para: Erase part or all of a flash memory or level it.

detaileddescription.para: If device is explicit erase type device or device driver provides erase callback, the callback of the device is called, in which it behaves the same way as flash_erase. If a device does not require explicit erase, either because it has no erase at all or has auto-erase/erase-on-write, and does not provide erase callback then erase is emulated by leveling selected device memory area with erase_value assigned to device.

Erase page offset and size are constrains of paged, explicit erase devices, but can be relaxed with devices without such requirement, which means that it is up to user code to make sure they are correct as the function will return on, if these constrains are not met, -EINVAL for paged device, but may succeed on non-explicit erase devices. For RAM non-volatile devices the erase pages are emulated, at this point, to allow smooth transition for code relying on device being paged to function properly; but this is completely software constrain.

Generally: if your code previously required device to be erase prior to some actions to work, replace flash_erase calls with this function; but if your code can work with non-volatile RAM type devices, without emulating erase, you should rather have different path of execution for page-erase, i.e. Flash, devices and call flash_erase for them.

parameterdescription.para: : size of area to be erased

simplesect.para: 0 on success, negative errno code on fail.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, off_t offset, struct flash_pages_info *info)
memberdef.name: flash_get_page_info_by_offs

briefdescription.para: Get the size and start offset of flash page at certain flash offset.

parameterdescription.para: Page Info structure to be filled

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -EINVAL if page of the offset doesn't exist.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint32_t page_index, struct flash_pages_info *info)
memberdef.name: flash_get_page_info_by_idx

briefdescription.para: Get the size and start offset of flash page of certain index.

parameterdescription.para: Index of the page. Index are counted from 0.
Page Info structure to be filled

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -EINVAL if page of the index doesn't exist.

briefdescription.para: Get the total number of flash pages.
Iterate over all flash pages on a device.

detaileddescription.para: This routine iterates over all flash pages on the given device, ordered by increasing start offset. For each page, it invokes the given callback, passing it the page's information and a private data object.

parameterdescription.para: Device whose pages to iterate over
Callback to invoke for each flash page

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, off_t offset, void *data, size_t len)
memberdef.name: flash_sfdp_read

briefdescription.para: Read data from Serial Flash Discoverable Parameters.

detaileddescription.para: This routine reads data from a serial flash device compatible with the JEDEC JESD216 standard for encoding flash memory characteristics.
Availability of this API is conditional on selecting CONFIG_FLASH_JESD216_API and support of that functionality in the driver underlying dev.

parameterdescription.para: device from which parameters will be read
address within the SFDP region containing data of interest
where the data to be read will be placed
the number of bytes of data to be read
if the flash driver does not support SFDP access

briefdescription.para: Read the JEDEC ID from a compatible flash device.

parameterdescription.para: device from which id will be read
pointer to a buffer of at least 3 bytes into which id will be stored
on successful store of 3-byte JEDEC id
if flash driver doesn't support this function

briefdescription.para: Get the minimum write block size supported by the driver.

detaileddescription.para: The write block size supported by the driver might differ from the write block size of memory used because the driver might implements write-modify algorithm.

briefdescription.para: Get pointer to flash_parameters structure.

detaileddescription.para: Returned pointer points to a structure that should be considered constant through a runtime, regardless if it is defined in RAM or Flash. Developer is free to cache the structure pointer or copy its contents.

simplesect.para: pointer to flash_parameters structure characteristic for the device.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint16_t code, const uintptr_t in, void *out)
memberdef.name: flash_ex_op

briefdescription.para: Execute flash extended operation on given device.

detaileddescription.para: Besides of standard flash operations like write or erase, flash controllers also support additional features like write protection or readout protection. These features are not available in every flash controller, what's more controllers can implement it in a different way.

It doesn't make sense to add a separate flash API function for every flash controller feature, because it could be unique (supported on small number of flash controllers) or the API won't be able to represent the same feature on every flash controller.

parameterdescription.para: Operation which will be executed on the device.
Pointer to input data used by operation. If operation doesn't need any input data it could be NULL.
Pointer to operation output data. If operation doesn't produce any output it could be NULL.
if given device doesn't support extended operation.
if support for extended operations is not enabled in Kconfig
negative value on extended operation errors.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *src_dev, off_t src_offset, const struct device *dst_dev, off_t dst_offset, off_t size, uint8_t *buf, size_t buf_size)
memberdef.name: flash_copy

briefdescription.para: Copy flash memory from one device to another.

detaileddescription.para: Copy a region of flash memory from one place to another. The source and destination flash devices may be the same or different devices. However, this function will fail if the source and destination devices are the same if memory regions overlap and are not identical.
The caller must supply a buffer of suitable size and ensure that the destination is erased beforehand, if necessary.

simplesect.para: If the source and destination devices are the same, and the source and destination offsets are also the same, this function succeeds without performing any copy operation.

parameterdescription.para: Offset within the source flash device.
Offset within the destination flash device.
Size of the region to copy, in bytes.
Pointer to a buffer of size buf_size.
Size of the buffer pointed to by buf.
if either src_dev or dst_dev are not ready.

briefdescription.para: Set for ordinary Flash where erase is needed before write of random data.
Reserved for users as initializer for variables that will later store capabilities.
Interfaces for flash memory controllers.
